page: Microsoft Azure D96plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz. Built on the arm64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server features 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 128 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 64 KB L1d cache. Storage is supported by a 5669 GB local disk with Premium Storage capabilities, while no GPU hardware is present.

In multi-core benchmarks, the D96plds_v6 demonstrates top-tier performance, particularly in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core metrics, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top tier for both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read and write operations. Single-core performance, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores, falls into the average tier. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier, alongside strong performance in software compilation with Clang.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server represents a compute-dense, low-memory hardware profile. It offers high qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded, CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. However, the lower memory-per-core ratio is a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. The server is highly suited for multi-threaded processing, software compilation, high-throughput static web serving, Redis caching, and database operations.
description: Microsoft Azure D96plds_v6 is an ARM-based server featuring 96 dedicated physical cores of the Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz. Operating on the arm64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it is configured with 192.0 GB of RAM, representing a compute-dense 2.0 GB per core ratio. The server includes 5669 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities but lacks GPU hardware. In benchmark testing, the D96plds_v6 delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database and Redis SET operation throughput. Conversely, single-core metrics remain in the average tier. This hardware profile provides high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy, multi-threaded workloads that do not demand extensive memory allocations. It is highly suited for software compilation, high-throughput static web serving, Redis caching, and database workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96plds_v6 is a 96-core ARM Neoverse-N2 server configured with 192 GB RAM and 5669 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance, high memory bandwidth, and database throughput.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96plds_v6 is a 96-core ARM Neoverse-N2 server with 192 GB RAM and 5669 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A compute-dense, 96-core ARM server featuring high-capacity local storage, optimized for multi-threaded processing and high-throughput database workloads.

192.0 GB system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core resource ratio
5669 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis SET performance
Best suited for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
